message.content:
Find all code comments (// and /* */ style) in the staged TypeScript/TSX/JS files. Run this command to get a list of files that contain comments along with the comment lines:

```
git diff --cached --name-only | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' | while read f; do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    # Find single-line comments (// ...) and multi-line comments (/* ... */)
    # Exclude URLs (http://, https://), shebang lines, and eslint directives
    matches=$(grep -nE '^\s*//' "$f" | grep -v 'http://' | grep -v 'https://' | grep -v '^#!' | head -50)
    matches2=$(grep -nE '^\s*/\*' "$f" | head -20)
    matches3=$(grep -nE '^\s*\*' "$f" | grep -v 'import' | head -20)
    if [ -n "$matches" ] || [ -n "$matches2" ]; then
      echo "=== $f ==="
      [ -n "$matches" ] && echo "$matches"
      [ -n "$matches2" ] && echo "$matches2"
      [ -n "$matches3" ] && echo "$matches3"
      echo ""
    fi
  fi
done
```

Also search for inline comments (code followed by // comment):
```
git diff --cached --name-only | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' | while read f; do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    # Find inline comments - lines with code then //
    matches=$(grep -nP '(?<!:)(?<!https?)//(?!/)\s' "$f" | grep -v 'http://' | grep -v 'https://' | head -30)
    if [ -n "$matches" ]; then
      echo "=== INLINE: $f ==="
      echo "$matches"
      echo ""
    fi
  fi
done
```

content.text:
⊰IUTP

I'll run both commands to find code comments in the staged TypeScript/TSX/JS files.

input.command:
git diff --cached --name-only | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' | while read f; do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    matches=$(grep -nE '^\s*//' "$f" | grep -v 'http://' | grep -v 'https://' | grep -v '^#!' | head -50)
    matches2=$(grep -nE '^\s*/\*' "$f" | head -20)
    matches3=$(grep -nE '^\s*\*' "$f" | grep -v 'import' | head -20)
    if [ -n "$matches" ] || [ -n "$matches2" ]; then
      echo "=== $f ==="
      [ -n "$matches" ] && echo "$matches"
      [ -n "$matches2" ] && echo "$matches2"
      [ -n "$matches3" ] && echo "$matches3"
      echo ""
    fi
  fi
done
input.description: Find single-line and multi-line comments in staged TS/TSX/JS files

input.command:
git diff --cached --name-only | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' | while read f; do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    matches=$(grep -nP '(?<!:)(?<!https?)//(?!/)\s' "$f" | grep -v 'http://' | grep -v 'https://' | head -30)
    if [ -n "$matches" ]; then
      echo "=== INLINE: $f ==="
      echo "$matches"
      echo ""
    fi
  fi
done
input.description: Find inline comments (code followed by //) in staged TS/TSX/JS files

213:    // Icon fonts (IconsSemParar, icomoon) + other local fonts — overrides DS on name collision

279:      // Native module mocks (no ~/ aliases here — handled by tildeResolverPlugin)

=== src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts ===
28:// FIXME: use something like "props: DUIProps<Foo, Bar>" during component
29://  creation in order to avoid eliminate the use of "as SDUIComponentRenderer"

=== src/services/sdui/models/index.ts ===
15:// ---- 3.5  JSON Logic (subset) --------------------------------------------
141:// ---- 3.1  Screen Structure ------------------------------------------------
158:// ---- Component Registry ---------------------------------------------------
172:// ---- Known Component Types (voucher screens) ------------------------------
17:/**
64:/**
18: * Accesses a value from the context using dot notation.
19: * @example { var: 'balance.pointsFormatted' }
20: */
65: * A boolean value computed from a JSON Logic rule.
66: * Used for visibility, enabled state, or any boolean prop.
67: * @example { rule: { '>': [{ var: 'form.points' }, 0] } }
68: */

=== src/services/sdui/renderer/FallbackComponent.tsx ===
10:/**
11: * Dev-only fallback for unregistered SDUI component types.
12: * Uses RN primitives intentionally — DS components require ThemeProvider
13: * which may not be available in all rendering contexts (tests, error boundaries).
14: */

=== src/services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer.test.tsx ===
364:      // Press should not throw even without actionRegistry

=== src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.test.ts ===
455:      // (points * 0.01) + 10
533:      // Build deeply nested: +(+(+(... 1, 1), 1), 1) — 25 levels deep
534:      // At depth > 20, computeArithmetic returns 0
535:      // Inner levels that exceed depth return 0, so the deepest operand
536:      // resolves to 0 instead of its true value, capping computation
541:      // Without depth limit: 2000 + 25*1000 = 27000
542:      // With depth limit at 20: innermost levels return 0, so result < 27000

content.text: The second command failed because macOS's BSD `grep` does not support `-P` (Perl regex). Let me re-run it with an extended regex approach instead.

input.command:
git diff --cached --name-only | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' | while read f; do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    matches=$(grep -nE '.+[^:]//[^/]' "$f" | grep -v '^\s*//' | grep -v 'http://' | grep -v 'https://' | head -30)
    if [ -n "$matches" ]; then
      echo "=== INLINE: $f ==="
      echo "$matches"
      echo ""
    fi
  fi
done
input.description: Find inline comments using extended regex compatible with macOS BSD grep

455:      // (points * 0.01) + 10
533:      // Build deeply nested: +(+(+(... 1, 1), 1), 1) — 25 levels deep
534:      // At depth > 20, computeArithmetic returns 0
535:      // Inner levels that exceed depth return 0, so the deepest operand
536:      // resolves to 0 instead of its true value, capping computation
541:      // Without depth limit: 2000 + 25*1000 = 27000
542:      // With depth limit at 20: innermost levels return 0, so result < 27000
